channel.title: Blog | 複合知能メディア研究室

item.title: 🧑‍🎓 For Prospective Students
item.description:
The Department of Media Intelligence (Nakashima Lab, or MIMLab) in SANKEN, The University of Osaka, accepts students as collaborative lab of the Department of Computer Science and Software Science, School of Engineering Science, The University of Osaka and the Graduate School of Information Science and Technology, The University of Osaka.

If you wish to join our lab as an undergraduate student, you must first enter through the Department of Information and Computer Sciences at the School of Engineering Science (Admissions Information).

If you wish to join as a graduate student, you must apply through the Graduate School of Information Science and Technology (Admissions Information). For both the Master’s and Doctoral programs, we encourage you to send your CV and a research proposal in advance to the email address here. This will allow us to conduct a screening process and and interview, through which we can provide various forms of support (Your CV and research proposal will be used solely for screening).

At our lab, we welcome students who are passionate about research and want to enjoy their student life while engaging in meaningful academic work. If you’re aiming to publish at top-tier conferences and are motivated to pursue high-level research, we would love for you to consider joining us. However, especially for graduate-level applicants, we place significant emphasis on prior research experience during the screening process.

We look forward to welcoming students who want to have fun while conducting exciting research!

item.title: 📄 A papers accepted to CVPR2026!
item.description:
A paper📄 is accepted to CVPR2026, one of the top conferences in Computer Vision🎊

  • Lu Wei, Yuta Nakashima, Noa Garcia (2026). EMMA: Concept Erasure Benchmark with Comprehensive Semantic Metrics and Diverse Categories, CVPR2026.

See you in Denver!

item.title: 📄 A apper accepted to ICLR2026!
item.description:
One paper📄 is accepted to ICLR2026, the top conference in Representation Learning🎊

  • Zongshang Pang, Mayu Otani, Yuta Nakashima (2026). Measure Twice, Cut Once: A Semantic-Oriented Approach to Video Temporal Localization with Video LLMs. ICLR 2026.

See you in Rio de Janeiro!

item.title: 📄 Two papers accepted to AAAI2026!
item.description:
Two papers📄📄 are accepted to AAAI2026, a top conference on artificial intelligence🎊

  • Liyun Zhang, Zheng Lian, Hong Liu, Takanori Takebe, Shozo Nishii, Yuta Nakashima (2026). QuMAB: Query-based Multi-annotator Behavior Pattern Learning. Proc. The Fortieth AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AAAI2026).
  • Liyun Zhang, Zheng Lian, Hong Liu, Takanori Takebe, Yuta Nakashima (2026). SimLabel: Similarity-Weighted Semi-supervision for Multi-annotator Learning with Missing Labels. Proc. The Fortieth AAAI Conference on Artificial Intelligence (AAAI2026).

item.title: 📄 Four papers accepted to ICCV2025!
item.description:
Four papers📄📄📄📄 are accepted to ICCV2025, one of the top conferences in Computer Vision🎊

  • Yusuke Hirota, Ryo Hachiuma, Boyi Li, Ximing Lu, Michael Ross Boone, Boris Ivanovic, Yejin Choi, Marco Pavone, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang, Noa Garcia, Yuta Nakashima, Chao-Han Huck Yang (2025). Bias in Gender Bias Benchmarks: How Spurious Features Distort Evaluation. Proc. the IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV2025).
  • Ryan Ramos, Yusuke Hirota, Yuta Nakashima, Noa Garcia (2025). From Global to Local: Social Bias Transfer in CLIP. Proc. the IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV2025) Workshop.
  • Ryan Ramos, Vladan Stojnić, Giorgos Kordopatis-Zilos, Yuta Nakashima, Giorgos Tolias, Noa Garcia (2025). Processing and acquisition traces in visual encoders: What does CLIP know about your camera? Proc. the IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV2025).
  • Bowen Wang, Zhouqiang Jiang, Yasuaki Susumu, Shotaro Miwa, Tianwei Chen, Yuta Nakashima (2025). Taming the Untamed: Graph-Based Knowledge Retrieval and Reasoning for MLLMs to Conquer the Unknown. Proc. the IEEE/CVF International Conference on Computer Vision (ICCV2025).

Our PI, Prof. Nakashima, also give an invited talk at 3rd Workshop on Scene Graphs and Graph Representation Learning (SG2RL) in conjunction with ICCV2025.

item.title: 📄 A paper accepted to ACL2025!
item.description:
Our paper 📄 is accepted to ACL2025, top conference in natural language processing🎊

  • Yusuke Hirota, Boyi Li, Ryo Hachiuma, Yueh-Hua Wu, Boris Ivanovic, Marco Pavone, Yejin Choi, Yu-Chiang Frank Wang, Yuta Nakashima, Chao-Han Huck Yang (2025). LOTUS: A Leaderboard for Detailed Image Captioning from Quality to Societal Bias and User Preferences. Proc. the 63rd Annual Meeting of the Association for Computational Linguistics (ACL2025).

item.title: ✨ Newcomers in 2025
item.description:
We have two new M1 students, four B4 students, and one FrontierLab student, who are the first student under the name of MIMLab.

Welcome aboard and enjoy your research projects!

item.title: 🔄 The Department of Intelligent Media
item.description:
With the retirement of Professor Yagi and the appointment of Professor Nakashima, the Department of Intelligent Media, SANKEN, The University of Osaka, has officially launched as the Nakashima Laboratory (MIMLab) as of April 1, 2025.

While inheriting the strong foundation built by Professor Yagi, we hope to create an environment where everyone can enjoy both research and other lab activities as part of this new chapter.
